findings.evidence: Grep shows only coefficient lemmas at lines 177 and 184, while the only support lemma is the integral special case at line 246: `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff`.
findings.fix: Add public lemmas such as `adicOrd_ne_zero_iff` and `mem_support_principalDivisor_ofDedekindDomain_iff : v ∈ ...support ↔ v.valuation K ((Additive.toMul u : Kˣ) : K) ≠ 1`, with the support lemma marked `@[simp]` if used as the normal form.
findings.issue: The public API characterizes coefficients of arbitrary Dedekind principal divisors but not their support; users cannot rewrite `v ∈ ((OrderSystem.ofDedekindDomain R K).principalDivisor u).support` to the valuation condition without unfolding through `Finsupp.mem_support_iff` and log facts.

findings.evidence: Line 246 declares `lemma mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff ...` with no attribute, unlike nearby normal-form lemmas such as `adicOrd_apply` at line 76 and `OrderSystem.ofDedekindDomain_ord` at line 171.
findings.fix: Mark `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff` as `@[simp]`; this rewrites from the constructed divisor support to ideal membership and has no reverse-looping shape.
findings.issue: `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff` is the advertised `mem_*_iff` characteristic lemma for divisors of integral elements, but it lacks a `@[simp]` annotation, so `simp` will not reduce support membership to `r ∈ v.asIdeal`.

summary: The concrete order system exposes the coefficient API, but the support API is incomplete and one advertised characteristic lemma is not available to automation.
